report.name: Marketing Spend vs Sales Revenue Correlation Analysis

chartData.Description: Low marketing spend with surprisingly strong sales performance
Moderate marketing investment yielding below-expected sales results
Higher marketing spend producing good sales, but inconsistent with pattern
Low spend with moderate sales - no clear relationship evident
High marketing investment with disappointing sales return
Mid-range spend achieving unexpectedly high sales performance
Maximum marketing spend failing to generate proportional sales
Modest investment with solid sales results showing no clear pattern
Significant spend producing below-average sales outcomes
Minimal marketing budget achieving strong sales through other factors
High spend with moderate sales - correlation questionable
Average marketing investment with poor sales performance

highlightsData.title: Weak Correlation Indicates Process Failure
highlightsData.content: Marketing spend shows virtually no correlation with sales revenue (R² = 0.12), indicating fundamental issues with marketing strategy, targeting, or execution. Current marketing investments appear to be ineffective and require comprehensive strategy overhaul.

attachments.name: weak_correlation_analysis.jpg
attachments.description: Analysis showing weak correlation and scattered data points

highlightsData.title: Random Distribution Suggests Missing Factors
highlightsData.content: Data points show random distribution rather than correlation pattern, suggesting marketing effectiveness depends on unmeasured variables like timing, targeting accuracy, competitive activity, or external market conditions not captured in current analysis.

attachments.name: missing_variables_analysis.jpg
attachments.description: Analysis identifying potential missing variables affecting marketing effectiveness

actionItems.title: Conduct Comprehensive Marketing Effectiveness Audit
actionItems.description: Perform detailed analysis of marketing campaigns, targeting accuracy, timing, and competitive factors. Identify why marketing spend fails to correlate with sales performance.

actionItems.title: Implement Marketing Attribution Tracking
actionItems.description: Deploy comprehensive tracking system to measure marketing campaign effectiveness. Include lead source tracking, conversion attribution, and customer journey analysis.

actionItems.title: Establish Marketing Performance Framework
actionItems.description: Create systematic approach to evaluate marketing ROI with clear metrics, attribution models, and performance thresholds. Implement monthly correlation analysis.
